name: Abu Sha'tha al-Kindi
title: Master Archer
shortDescription: Yazid ibn Ziyad (Abu Sha'tha), a skilled archer from Kufa who came with Umar Sa'd's army but joined the Imam and fired his arrows at the enemy.
fullStory:
Yazid ibn Ziyad, known as Abu Sha'tha al-Kindi, was a skilled archer and renowned warrior of Kufa. Initially, he left Kufa with Umar Sa'd's army to confront Imam Hussain (AS) and came to Karbala.

When Abu Sha'tha saw that Ibn Ziyad and Umar Sa'd rejected the Imam's peaceful proposals and intended to kill the Prophet's (PBUH) son, his conscience awakened. He decided to defect from the army of falsehood and join the caravan of truth.

On the day of Ashura, Abu Sha'tha, who was a highly skilled archer, stood next to Imam Hussain (AS) and took out a hundred arrows from his quiver. He knelt down and with utmost precision, fired the arrows one after another at the Kufan army.

Imam Hussain (AS), seeing his valor, prayed for him, saying: 'O God, guide his shots to the target and make his reward Paradise.' Abu Sha'tha brought down many enemy forces with his arrows.

After running out of arrows, he drew his sword and charged manfully into the heart of the enemy until he was martyred by sword and spear strikes, achieving a blessed end.
quoteArabic: اللّهُمَّ سَدِّدْ رَمْيَتَهُ وَاجْعَلْ ثَوابَهُ الجَنَّةَ
quote: (The Imam's prayer for him:) O God, guide his shots to the target and make his reward Paradise.
